name: USS Tills (DE 748)
events: 21 Oct 1944 From 21 to 23 October 1944, USS Archerfish (T/Cdr. J.F. Enright, USN), USS Kete (T/Cdr. R.L. Rutter, USN) and USS Sea Cat (T/Cdr. R.R. McGregor, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor with an inbound convoy coming from San Diego. This convoy was made up of the escort carriers USS Makin Island (T/Capt. W.B. Whaley, Jr., USN), USS Lunga Point (T/Capt. G.A.T. Washburn, USN), USS Bismarck Sea (T/Capt. J.L. Pratt, USN) and USS Salamaua (T/Capt. J.I. Taylor, Jr., USN). They were escorted since departure from San Diego by the destroyer escort USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR). The escort had been reinforced by the destroyer escorts USS Connolly (Lt.Cdr. W.A. Collier, USNR) and USS Bright (Lt.Cdr. W.A. McMahan, USNR) which had escorted the three submarines from Pearl Harbor to the rendezvous point with the incoming convoy.

3 Nov 1944 USS Blenny (Cdr. W.H. Hazzard, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR), USS Hemminger (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Bodler, USNR), USS Viking, USS ATR-26 (Lt. J.H. Kelly, USN), USS Plaice (Cdr. C.B. Stevens, Jr., USN) and USS Spikefish (Cdr. N.J. Nicholas, USN).
5 Nov 1944 USS Blenny (Cdr. W.H. Hazzard, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR) and USS Hemminger (Lt.Cdr. J.R. Bodler, USNR).

6 Nov 1944 USS Blenny (Cdr. W.H. Hazzard, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR) and USS Vammen (Lt.Cdr. L.M. King, Jr., USNR), USS Spikefish (Cdr. N.J. Nicholas, USN) and USS Sea Owl (Cdr. C.L. Bennett, USN).

7 Nov 1944 USS Blenny (Cdr. W.H. Hazzard, USN) conducted exercises off Pearl Harbor together with USS Tills (Lt.Cdr. J.L. Brooks, USNR), USS Harmon (Lt.Cdr. T.U. Weekes, USNR), USS ATR-26 (Lt. J.H. Kelly, USN) and USS Spikefish (Cdr. N.J. Nicholas, USN).
